text.title: testing-with-assistive-technology Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-with-assistive-technology as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent declares "WCAG AA conformant" based on axe-core results alone.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automated tools catch 30–50% of real issues. Claiming conformance requires documented testing against all applicable success criteria, including manual SR checks. Always reject this conclusion in prompt rules.

failure-mode.description: LLM inventing ARIA patterns without citation to ARIA Authoring Practices Guide.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invented ARIA patterns produce non-standard keyboard behavior and incorrect SR announcements. Every ARIA pattern must cite a specific APG example URL; refuse output without citation.

failure-mode.description: Color-contrast checks performed via static analysis on overlapping translucent layers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM static analysis cannot compute effective contrast of translucent overlapping elements. Must run in browser against the rendered DOM.

failure-mode.description: Testing only initial render state for dynamic content (aria-live, SPA route changes).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dynamic content is the primary false-pass area. Focus must be tested post-state: after dialog opens, after route change, after async content loads.
